title: chamaecytisus hirsutus subsp. leucotrichus
intro: Chamaecytisus hirsutus subsp. leucotrichus, also known as the hairy broom or woolly broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. This shrub-like plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Chamaecytisus hirsutus subsp. leucotrichus include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
